preamble: The transformation of a voiceless bilabial plosive /p/ into the diphthong /uo/ is an unusual but attested phonetic shift, primarily occurring through a series of lenition and vocalization processes. This shift involves the consonant losing its plosive articulation, potentially becoming a fricative or approximant, and then vocalizing to a vowel, which subsequently forms part of a diphthong.
sources: Coetsem, F. van, & Buccini, A. F. (1990). Variation and the reconditioning of phonological rules: Cases from Germanic and Romance. Lingua: International Review of General Linguistics, 81(2-3), 169-220.
